Tests if dividing NaN by positive zero results in NaN using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of dividing `Number.NaN` by positive zero (`+0`) in JavaScript. The `assert` function is used to verify that the result of this operation is `NaN`, which is expected to evaluate to `true` when passed to the `isNaN` function. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles the division of `NaN` by zero, which is a specific edge case in numerical computations. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a single mathematical operation and its expected outcome.
